description: MCP tool routing tests — natural language prompt → expected tool name (null = no tool should fire).
notes: Each test has a prompt (user intent in natural English) and an expected tool name.
The Copilot workflow reads this file and the @mcp.tool() docstrings in src/lab_registry/server.py,
then decides the routing WITHOUT looking at 'expected', and compares afterwards.
'expected': null means the prompt is out of scope for this MCP server entirely.
When multiple tools could apply, 'expected' names the most specific/correct one.

tests.prompt: List all entries available in the gen-e2 registry
Show me all agents in the registry
List all skills from the Android plugin
What gen-e2 plugins are available?
How many skills, agents, commands and plugins does the gen-e2 marketplace have?
Find skills or agents about tdd
Search for anything with 'architecture' in the name
I need to review architecture and write ADRs for my project, what should I use?
I am building a Go microservice and need TDD support. Which gen-e2 plugins are relevant?
I'm starting a new Android app. Which gen-e2 plugins should I install?
Get the full content of the android-architecture skill from the android plugin
Fetch android/skill/android-architecture
Show me everything in the research-suite plugin
I want to install the delivery plugin into my project
What changed in the android plugin between versions?
I have android at version 0.1.0 and delivery at version 1.2.0 installed locally. Are they up to date with the registry?

tests.intent: compliance — check full plugin by version
tests.prompt: I have the android plugin installed at version 0.1.0. Is it up to date?
Validate this skill markdown file against the gen-e2 schema
Reload the registry after the latest marketplace update
Get the instructions for the add-screen command in the android plugin
Deploy my application to production
Write me a unit test for my Python function
What is the weather in Paris today?
Run git commit and push my changes
